fields.NOT_SELL_1: Do not lead with model types or ML architecture details.
fields.NOT_SELL_2: Do not pitch AI before confirming real production use cases.
fields.NOT_SELL_3: Do not advance without understanding how AI fits existing workflows.
fields.NOT_SELL_LENS: Qualify out on experimental AI interest, unclear use cases, and isolated ML projects.

fields.NOT_SELL_1: Do not start with cluster architecture or shard configuration.
fields.NOT_SELL_2: Do not assume search is a problem without user impact.
fields.NOT_SELL_3: Do not advance without confirming relevance or performance issues.
fields.NOT_SELL_LENS: Qualify out on small datasets, simple apps, and low search importance.

fields.NOT_SELL_1: Do not lead with dashboards or visualization features.
fields.NOT_SELL_2: Do not pitch observability without incident or outage pain.
fields.NOT_SELL_3: Do not advance without understanding current monitoring limitations.
fields.NOT_SELL_LENS: Qualify out on simple systems, low incident frequency, and limited operational complexity.

fields.NOT_SELL_1: Do not start with detection rule libraries or technical features.
fields.NOT_SELL_2: Do not pitch SIEM replacement without understanding SOC workflows.
fields.NOT_SELL_3: Do not advance without confirming alert volume or investigation pain.
fields.NOT_SELL_LENS: Qualify out on immature security programs, small teams, and low monitoring needs.

fields.NOT_SELL_1: Do not pitch platform vision before identifying consolidation goals.
fields.NOT_SELL_2: Do not assume customers want fewer tools without confirming cost pressure.
fields.NOT_SELL_3: Do not advance without understanding current data platform complexity.
fields.NOT_SELL_LENS: Qualify out on satisfied tool stacks, small environments, and limited data scale.
